observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:


     a. Municipal Assessor to prepare and furnish the  Municipal  Treasurer
        on or before the 31st day of December each year, an assessment roll
        containing a list of all persons whose real  properties  have  been
        newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such  properties  as
        basis for updating the RPT Account Registers (RPTARs);


     b. Municipal Treasurer to prepare yearly  a  duly  certified  list  of
        taxpayers based on the updated RPTARs  showing  the  names  of  the
        taxpayers and the amount due and  collectible  for  the  year,  and
        furnish  the  same  to  the  Municipal  Accounting  Office  at  the
        beginning of every fiscal year;


     c. Municipal Accountant to record RPT/SET Receivable yearly  based  on
        the duly certified list provided by the MTO;


     d. Municipal Accountant to prepare  and  maintain  the  JEVs  and  the
        Abstract of RPT Collections in accordance with  the  provisions  of
        GAAM and Manual on the NGAS for LGUs;


     e.  Municipal  Accountant  to  observe  due  diligence  in   recording
        transactions by using the proper accounts as provided under Annex B
        of COA Circular No. 2015-009  and  ensuring  sufficient  basis  and
        documentation in order to come  up  with  accurate  and  faithfully
        represented FS; and


     f.  Municipal  Accountant  and  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to  conduct
        periodic reconciliation of their records in order to come  up  with
        accurate and reliable reports on the levy of RPT and SET.

We recommended the Management to:

   a. Require that reconciliation between the records of the MA and  GSO  be
      undertaken and effect the  necessary  adjustments  in  the  accounting
      books to fairly present the said accounts in the financial statements;
   b. perform One-Time Cleansing of PPE as prescribed in COA Circular  2020-
      006 to be able to establish PPE balances that  are  verifiable  as  to
      existence, condition and accountability;

   c. require the GSO to maintain the pertinent reports and property records
      of Road Networks to be able to provide reliable basis on the amounts
      presented in the financial statements;

   d. require Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to FS the road
      network system;

   e. dispose the unserviceable properties and  the  corresponding  cost  or
      value be dropped from the books of accounts;

   f. direct the Municipal Accountant to identify possible items that  needs
      to be excluded from the PPE account and  make  proper  adjustments  if
      deemed necessary; and


   g. ensure that an ICS is on file or had been prepared by the  GSO  before
      these semi-expendable assets are removed from PPE  account  to  ensure
      control and accountability.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

      a. locate/trace/pinpoint possible errors in recording that caused  the
         variances and prepare necessary adjusting entries in the respective
         funds to reflect their correct and reliable  balances  as  at  year
         end;


      b. conduct regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts to  eliminate
         the difference; and


      c.  prepare  the  subsidiary  ledgers   with   complete   details   of
         transactions to provide  ready  reference  for  reconciliation  and
         verification purposes.

We recommended that Management:

     a.  require  the  concerned  officers  and  employees  to  immediately
        settle/liquidate  their  outstanding  cash  advances  through   the
        submission of duly  accomplished  liquidation  reports,  otherwise,
        enforce  the  withholding  of  salary,  in  accordance   with   the
        provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002;


     b. stop the grant of additional cash advances to  all  personnel  with
        unliquidated cash advances until such time previous  cash  advances
        are fully settled, and see to it that officials and  employees  who
        seek transfer to other offices or those filing for terminal  leaves
        are not given certificates of clearance until  their  balances  are
        paid;

     c. request for the write-off of dormant accounts  in  accordance  with
        COA Circular No. 2016-005, if applicable;

     d. prepare and maintain complete and updated  SLs,  ageing  and  other
        subsidiary  records  of  the  accounts  presented  in  the  FS   in
        accordance with Section 10 of the Manual  on  the  NGAS  for  LGUs,
        Volume II;

     e. monitor and establish controls to ensure the timely liquidation  of
        cash advances through updated monthly ageing of the same to prevent
        accumulation of unliquidated cash  advances  and  ensure  that  the
        rules and regulations on the grant of cash  advances  are  strictly
        observed.

We recommended that Management require the:

     a. GSO-designate/Inventory Committee to prepare and submit a  complete
        Report on the Physical Count of Inventories per type as of December
        31, 2021 in order to determine  the  correctness  of  the  recorded
        inventories in the books of accounts of  the  Municipality  and  to
        ensure  that  semestral  count  of  inventory  items  is   promptly
        conducted and the corresponding report is submitted on time to  the
        Audit Team;


     b. establishment of a separate  General  Services  Office  (GSO)  with
        stock room for  proper  storage  of  inventories  and  require  the
        coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the  GSO  towards
        the full  implementation  of  the  perpetual  inventory  method  in
        recording of supplies and materials;

     c. prepare and maintain complete and updated  general  and  subsidiary
        ledgers of accounts presented in the financial statements;

     d. Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry for  the  proper
        classification of goods for  distribution  to  people  affected  by
        calamities/disasters by debiting “Welfare Goods  for  Distribution”
        account amounting to ₱100,000.00 and crediting “Food  Supplies  for
        Distribution”  account  by  the  same  amount,  and  ensure  proper
        classification   of   accounts   in   succeeding   procurement   of
        inventories; and


     e. come up with internal control procedures that  will  ensure  proper
        handling of inventories to prevent or minimize the risk of loss and
        misuse in accordance with the provisions of the Manual on the  NGAS
        for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Management: (a) direct  the  Municipal  Social
Welfare Development Office, Municipal Health Office  and  Accounting  Office
to account for  the  distribution  of  the  above  mentioned  goods  to  the
recipient beneficiaries and to immediately submit the report  thereon  as  a
basis for confirmation and validation of the funds disbursed;  (b)  strictly
adhere with the documentary requirements provided by  rules  and  regulation
to prevent adverse audit findings; (c)  submit  the  lacking  documents  and
ensure completeness, accuracy, and propriety  of  documents  supporting  the
claims before processing the payment to prevent suspension  or  disallowance
in audit; (d) strictly comply with the posting requirements set by the  GPPB
as mandated by the government; and (e) direct the BAC to transact only  with
suppliers who are  technically,  financially  and  legally  capable  and  to
always ensure that the most  advantageous  price  for  the  Municipality  is
obtained.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to: (a)
record the confirmed bank balances of the four bank  accounts  of  the  LGU;
and (b) set-up, maintain, and prepare individual BRS and subsidiary  ledgers
for these bank accounts to avoid understatement of the CIB-LCCA  account  at
any given date.

We recommended that Management:

   a. direct the GSO to ensure that the inventory of original OR/CR  of  the
      Municipality’s vehicles should be under his custody; and


   b. direct the GSO to regularly monitor  the  expiration  and  renewal  of
      registration of all motor vehicles.

We recommended that Management: (a) require the Accountant to  provide
complete documentation and adequate disclosure of the payment for  financial
assistance/subsidies for conduct of purported barangay activities  to  avoid
suspension of the transaction in audit; (b) ensure that all checks  covering
the  aid/financial  assistance/subsidy  be   deposited   directly   to   the
authorized depository account of the barangay;  (c) instruct  the  Municipal
Accountant and Barangay Record Keeper and/or  Barangay  Treasurer  that  all
aid/financial   assistance/subsidy   aid/financial   assistance/subsidy   be
properly receipted, recorded and accounted in their respective books.

We recommended that the Management direct  the  Offices  concerned  to
ensure the full implementation of all projects funded by 20% DF for  optimum
utilization of  the  fund  to  help  achieve  the  desirable  socio-economic
development and environmental outcomes of the LGU.

      We also  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Planning  and  Development
Officer (MPDO) closely  monitor  and  evaluate  the  implementation  of  the
different development  projects  in  accordance  with  the  AIP  for  timely
delivery  of  basic  and  value-added   services   and   benefits   to   its
constituents.

We recommended that  management:  (a)  utilize  the  20  per  cent  DF
exclusively  for  development  projects  that  promote  the  attainment   of
desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes; (b) provide  sufficient
allocation in the General Fund  for  operating  requirements  of  the  solid
waste management programs and  the  KALAHI  Office;  and (c)  refrain  from
including in the appropriation of development  projects  the  expenses  that
are not eligible charges against the 20 per cent Development Fund.

We recommended that Management: (a) direct the MDRRMO to  continue  to
monitor the implementation of all LDRRM PPAs to fully  achieve  the  desired
benefits therefrom; (b) direct all Municipal officials to judiciously  plan,
allocate and implement PPAs under the 5%  LDRRMF  solely  and  strictly  for
disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness,  response,  rehabilitation
and recovery purposes to ensure maximum utilization of  fund  in  compliance
with existing law, rules and regulations.

We recommended that management  require  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
facilitate disbursement for scholarships of the LGU scholars through  direct
payment to the school where the grantee was enrolled and  stop  the  payment
thru cash advance.

We recommended that  Management  direct  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
conduct and intensify tax campaigns to maximize  collections  especially  on
the delinquent taxpayers and impose remedies provided for, and exercise  the
taxing authority as mandated under the Local Government Code.

We recommended that Management:

     a. require the different offices/end-users in  the  agency  to  submit
        their Project  Procurement  Management  Plan  (PPMP)  incorporating
        therein all their procurement requirements for the  year  and  have
        them  duly  evaluated  and  reviewed  for  consolidation   into   a
        comprehensive, workable and realistic Annual Procurement Plan (APP)
        and follow the procurement methods and activities as stated in said
        APP;


     b. strictly comply with the provisions enumerated in the  Revised  IRR
        of RA No. 9184 and GPPB Resolution No. 11-2020; and


     c. whenever practicable, consider consolidation of  purchase  requests
        for commonly used supplies needed  by  the  different  offices  and
        adopt bulk purchases  to  promote  efficiency  in  the  procurement
        process.

We recommended that Management: (a) discontinue the practice of paying
in advance  for  services  not  yet  rendered;  (b)  require  the  immediate
submission of pertinent documents such as complete menu  list  and  pictures
of the actual foods served by the caterer during  the  event  including  the
unit cost of each meal to  establish  the  validity  and  propriety  of  the
claim; (c) strictly adhere  to  the  rules  and  regulations  set  forth  in
Section 88 of PD 1445.

We recommended that Management: (a) refrain  from  conducting  out-of-
town trainings/seminars/other LGU  activities  unless  extremely  necessary,
and instead use LGU in-house facilities or nearby  establishments  as  venue
for holding such activity to consider the prudent and judicious  utilization
of government funds; (b) conduct prior  assessment  and  evaluation  of  the
trainings/seminars/activities     to     be     conducted,     participants’
qualifications, training needs,  and  available  resources  of  the  LGU  to
ensure that only those trainings, or  seminars  that  are  highly  relevant,
mandatory, and responsive to the capacity-building needs or requirements  of
the LGU are attended to or participated in  by  varied  and  most  qualified
people, to avoid any wasteful and inefficient utilization  of  scarce  funds
of the LGU; (c) strictly comply  with  the  above  regulation  to  safeguard
government funds against wastage and improper disposition.

We recommended that management: (a) prepare specific and  well-defined
guidelines on the AICs program of  the  Municipality  to  ensure  that  only
eligible and qualified indigent beneficiaries are  granted  the  assistance;
(b) require the  Municipal  Accountant  to  provide  complete  and  adequate
documentation on the LGUs provision of assistance to individuals  in  crisis
situation mentioned  above;  (c)  institutionalize  the  grant  of  AICs  of
financial assistance through MSWDO which is the Office mandated  to  conduct
prior study, evaluation, and recommendation of qualified recipients  of the
program;  (d)  initiate  a  comprehensive  review  of  the  LGUs   financial
assistance program to  indigents  individuals  and  families  and  implement
reforms so that AICs program benefits are extended to as many  eligible  and
needy clients as possible.

We recommended  that  Management:  (a)  designate  Special  Disbursing
Officer at the MSWDO who will handle cash  advances  for  AICs;  (b)  direct
MSWDO that financial assistance to indigent beneficiaries be  given  in  the
form of outright cash, instead of direct payment in check to facilitate  the
distribution of assistance to needy clients at the earliest  possible  time;
(c) ensure that policies adopted by the MSWD are aligned with the  pertinent
provisions of DSWD issuances on social welfare and development.

We recommended that management: a) exercise restraint and prudence  in
the utilization of scarce government resources for meals, snacks, and  other
food items consumed or  served  during  LGU  sponsored  activities,  events,
and/or meetings; b) judiciously plan all  official  activities,  events  and
programs that demand food and representation expenses, taking  into  account
the necessity and reasonableness of food provision,  the  reasonable  number
of participants, and the timing and frequency  of  meetings  and  activities
undertaken; c) require that procurement of  meals,  snacks  and  other  food
items be supported with proper and complete documentation to  establish  the
propriety and validity of the claims; and (d) stop the practice of  spending
public funds on meals and  snacks  served  during  ordinary  management  and
legislative  meetings  and  on  consumable  grocery  and  food   items   for
consumption, entertainment, or reception of staff and guests.

We recommended that Management: (a) instruct the GAD  focal  person  /
GFPs to monitor the implementation  of  the  identified  PPAs  and  maximize
utilization of the GAD funds  to  accomplish  the  purposes/objectives   for
which the GAD  PPAs  were  identified   to  deliver  immediate  and  optimum
benefits to target beneficiaries; (b) ensure that GAD Accomplishment  Report
are prepared based on the GPB approved by the DILG,  and any  changes  made
therein be properly communicated  in  writing  to  the  oversight  body  and
attach all  pertinent  documents  relative  thereto;  (c)  revisit  internal
policies and procedures on GAD and implement a well-defined  monitoring  and
evaluation system to properly assess GAD PPAs responsiveness in relation  to
gender mainstreaming.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant thoroughly reconcile  the
Due to GSIS account and ensure that  all  premiums/loan  payment  are  fully
remitted to the GSIS within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Management instruct the  Municipal  Accountant
to cause  the  immediate  remittance  to  Pag-IBIG  or  make  the  necessary
adjustment in the books of accounts as the case maybe.

      Henceforth, we recommended that the Management require  the  Municipal
Accountant to ensure that all Pag-IBIG contributions  and/or  loan  payments
withheld from officials and employees are remitted on time.

We recommended that Management: (a) ensure the  consistencies  of  10-
Year Solid Waste Management Plan with the National  Solid  Waste  Management
Framework; (b) prioritize the development of the sanitary landfill in  order
to stop the operation of  controlled  dumpsite;  (c)  strictly  enforce  the
Mandatory  Segregation  of  Solid  Wastes  policy  of  RA  9003  to  achieve
minimization and reduction of  wastes;  (d)  assist  all  barangays  in  the
establishment of MRF; and (e) reconstitute  the  MSWMB  to  conform  to  the
membership requirements of the Act and to optimize the involvement of  civil
society and other sectors as envisioned in the law.

We recommended that the LCE direct the LFC to immediately post in the LGU’s website and to at least three conspicuous places within the locality the accumulated Monthly Reports on Fund Utilization and Status of PPA Implementation of the Financial Assistance to LGUs amounting to  ₱10.2 million.

We recommended that the LCE require the LDRRM Council to monitor consistently and evaluate regularly the implementation of PPAs to ensure that utilization of all funds are maximized in support to the disaster risk reduction and management activities of the municipality.

We recommended that the LCE strictly enforce compliance with the requirements set forth in the provisions of COA Circular No. 2007-001. Further, require the MA to record the grant of financial assistance to NGOs under the Due from NGOs/POs Account and require the liquidation thereof.

We commended Management for the substantial implementation of developmental projects as provided under JMC No. 2020-1 dated November 4, 2020. Nevertheless, we recommended that Management to strive for the total implementation of all projects embodied in the 20% Development Plan.

We recommended that the LCE require the LDRRM officer and other concerned agency officials to be resolute in project implementation, and monitor and evaluate regularly the implementation of PPAs in compliance with Rule 4, Section 1 of the IRR of RA 10121 to ensure that preparedness, mitigation and rehabilitation projects are implemented in the corresponding budget year to avoid, lessen or limit the potential adverse impact of disasters.

We recommended that the LCE direct all concerned employees to settle their unliquidated cash advances for travel in accordance with Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 97-002.  See to it that all cash advances are liquidated at year-end.

We recommended that Management:
Provide a stock room for proper storage of items of inventories and require the coordination between the MA and the GSO towards the implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording of supplies and materials;
Require the GSO to prepare the SSMIs weekly supported with RIS for supplies and materials issued and the MA to prepare JEV as supported by SSMIs and RIS submitted by the GSO to record issuance of supplies and materials; and
Require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of supplies and materials semestrally and submit the Physical Inventory Report to the Auditor’s Office as required in Section 124, Volume 1 of the MNGAs.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the LCE direct the MA to follow the proper process in recording RPT and SET Receivables pursuant to the provisions prescribed under Section 20, Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended the Management to:
Prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis; and
Perform constant review of recorded transactions to deter error and omissions.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA to review and analyze the long outstanding Guaranty/Security Deposits Payables and coordinate with concerned personnel for proper disposition and, if warranted, release immediately the retention money to the concerned suppliers in accordance with Section 62.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.   However, if suppliers could not be located anymore thereby constituting the retained amounts as invalid claims, these should be reverted back to un-appropriated surplus.

We recommended that the LCE require the MT to develop an effective Tax Collection Campaign program to intensify and maximize collection of real property taxes. Enforce the deputation of barangay treasurers to aid Municipality in the collection of taxes.
We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC to ensure compliance with posting requirements for procurement under emergency cases pursuant to Section 54.3 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor to update and
reconcile their records to determine the actual number of lots owned by the
Municipality, their correct valuation in accordance with accounting
standards, their whereabouts and other necessary information;
b) Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Engineer to conduct
ocular inspection and survey of the parcels of land owned by the
Municipality to determine their status, their actual possessor, and
possible boundary issues;
c) Issue a memorandum order for the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal
Assessor and Municipal Administrator to cause the registration and transfer
of title of the lands acquired by the Municipality; and
d) Require the Municipal Accountant to record the parcels of Land presented
in Table 2.

Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Assessor make representation with
the Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate funds for the expenses in the processing of
the Transfer Certificate of Title.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) require the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Inventory, Inspection and
Appraisal Form and submit to the Audit Team for validation in the case
of properties costing below P100,000.00 and referral to COA Regional
Director for inspection on items costing more than P100,000.00.
b) require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to
recompute the cost of the damaged properties based on the prescribed
formula provided by the DBM as contained in the Manual on Disposal of
Government Property.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MDRRM Officer to
submit to the Municipal Accountant the distribution list as the latter’s basis to draw
a JEV to recognize the cost of the items distributed to the victims of Typhoon
Auring and Typhoon Odette.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) reorganize the Inventory Committee and require the same to conduct physical inventory and render report thereon as required under Section 124, Chapter 7, Volume I of the NGAS Manual. The progress of the inventory taking should be monitored by the Local Chief Executive.

Thereafter, the Committee, the General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office reconcile all the inventory reports with the books of accounts.

Require the designated Property Officer to maintain property cards and update them periodically to aid in the conduct of physical count and to solve or minimize the perennial problem of unreconciled Accounting and Property records; and

Require the previous Chairmen of the Inventory Committee to explain their failure to conduct inventory and render report thereon. Likewise, the Committee should be warned that failure to perform their duties as Chairman and Members of the Inventory Committee may be a valid ground for filing of administrative charges for gross neglect of duty.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to remit all unspent funds for KR 2 and MGP projects to the National Treasury so that the same could be used to finance other priority projects of the National Government. After the remittance, require him to effect adjustments in the books in accordance with the provision of COA Circular 97-001 dated February 5, 2015.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

a. The Local Chief Executive (LCE) reorganize the Inventory Committee and
require the same to conduct physical inventory and render report thereon
as required under Section 124, Chapter 7, Volume I of the NGAS Manual.
The progress of the inventory taking should be monitored by the LCE;
b. Thereafter, the Committee, the General Services Office (GSO) and the
Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) shall reconcile all the inventory
reports with the books of accounts;
c. Require the designated Property Officer to maintain property cards and
update them periodically to aid in the conduct of physical count and to
solve or minimize the perennial problem of unreconciled Accounting and
Property records; and
d. Require the previous Chairmen of the Inventory Committee to explain
their failure to conduct inventory and render report thereon. Likewise, the
Committee should be warned that failure to perform their duties as
Chairman and Members of the Inventory Committee may be a valid
ground for filing of administrative charges for gross neglect of duty.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MA to record and adjust the
identified book reconciling items. Also, we recommended that the LCE instruct the
Municipal Treasurer (MT) and the MA to close immediately the dormant accounts
for KR 2 and MGP and remit the remaining funds to the source agencies, if
identified, otherwise remit the same to the Bureau of Treasury.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA to coordinate with the
concerned servicing banks for the timely submission of the bank statements with
supporting documents and to submit for audit the BRS on a regular basis pursuant to
COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MA to follow the proper process in
recognizing and setting-up RPT and SET Receivables pursuant to the provisions
prescribed under Section 20, Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that:

a. The LCE direct the MA, the MT and the implementing departments to
confirm whether the projects charged to particular trust funds were
actually completed and determine whether there are any other outstanding
obligations chargeable to the funds. If projects were already completed
and no other outstanding obligations accrue to the funds, return
immediately the unexpended balance to SA. If the SA cannot be identified,
remit immediately the remaining idle and unutilized funds to the Bureau of
Treasury.
b. For prior year unreconciled balances, the MA shall continue to conduct
reconciliation and effect the necessary adjustments in the FS.

We recommended that the LCE:
1. Direct the MA not to process request for CAs and DVs relative to
procurement transactions which are not in consonance with the prescribed
laws, rules and regulations; and
2. Require the Municipal GS Officer and the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) to adhere strictly to the prescribed laws and regulations in the
procurement of goods and services.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA to prepare the necessary
correcting entries for the cash donations received amounting to ₱0.67 million under
the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and the unutilized balance of ₱181 cash
donations for COVID 19.

In addition, management shall ensure that donations received intended for
risk reduction and management fund are properly accounted in the Trust Fund
Books.

We recommended that the LCE:
1. Direct the MSWD Officer to submit a written justification on the
observations noted above. If determined to be ineligible, cause the
immediate refund of the amount paid to the beneficiaries informing them
through writing. Upon receipt of the refunded amounts, return the said
amounts to the Bureau of Treasury; and
2. Ensure that proper verification and validation of the targeted beneficiaries
are done in the future to ensure that ESP and other similar benefits will be
granted to duly qualified recipients observing the prohibitions stated in
DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09, Series of 2020.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal GS Officer to submit
promptly to COA copies of contracts, purchase orders and notices of deliveries
pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 96-010.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer prepare and submit immediately
to the AT the report on the sources and utilization of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the LSB prepare an optimal and timely SEF Budget
by:

a) utilizing effectively the prescribed planning and budgeting tools provided in
DepEd, DBM & DILG Joint Circular No. 2017-1; and
b) observing strictly the timelines in the planning and budgeting process as
prescribed by the same Circular.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer, Municipal
Planning Development Officer and other concerned LGU officials to:

a. monitor closely and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority
development projects and programs under the 20% DF targeted to attain
social, economic and environmental development in LGU - Bato; and
b. observe strictly the timelines in the implementation of infrastructure
projects to guarantee prompt completion thereof for the benefit of the
general public.

We recommended that the LCE direct the HRMO and MTO to remit
immediately the outstanding contributions and loan repayments due to Pag-IBIG to
avoid further penalties and ensure that benefits can be readily availed of by the
officers and personnel members. Hence, timely remittance of mandatory
contributions and loan repayments is necessary to ensure that employees will receive
the benefits intended for them and for the LGU to avoid incurring additional
expense.

We also recommended that the HRMO and MTO ensure strict compliance to
prescribed deadlines set by the GSIS and HDMF on the remittance of monthly
withheld contributions and loans.

We recommended that the designated GSO to consolidate the weekly RIS in the SSMI for CY 2019 and previous years transactions, and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant together with the original copies of RIS;

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary JEVs based on the submitted SSMIs and record the expenditures under the Prior Period Adjustment account pursuant to Section 121 of NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommended that the LCE direct all employees with unliquidated cash advances to settle immediately cash advances for official travels in accordance with Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 97-002.  See to it that all cash advances are liquidated at year-end.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all concerned personnel to comply with the preparation of records/reports prescribed under COA Circular 2015-008.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to review immediately the balances of the inter-agency payable accounts to determine the LGU’s actual liability to the concerned government agencies, and prepare the appropriate adjustments.

If the SA cannot be identified, remit immediately the remaining idle and unutilized funds to the Bureau of Treasury.

For prior year unreconciled balances, the Municipal Accountant shall continue to conduct reconciliation and effect the necessary adjustments in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE, being the Head of the Local School Board direct the Municipal General Services Officer and the BAC to observe that procurement of goods should be done through public bidding; other modes of procurement as provided under R. A. 9184 could be resorted to if the public bidding is not applicable. All transactions pertaining to procurement should be evaluated by the Bids and Awards Committee, and a corresponding resolution thereto should be prepared and attached to the corresponding disbursement voucher in consonance with the provision of Section 12 of RA No. 9184.

Also, we recommended that the LCE require the Accountant not to process disbursement vouchers relative to procurement transactions without the necessary documents and approved BAC resolutions to ensure that all transactions have undergone BAC evaluation.

We recommended that the LCE strictly direct the BAC Secretariat to prepare a consolidated and approved APP in compliance with Sections 7.3.4, 7.3.5 and 14.1(i) of RA No. 9184 to facilitate the procurement process and delivery of goods, services to end-users and immediate implementation of infrastructure projects for the benefit of intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Local Finance Committee to conduct a semi-annual review and general examination of expenses and accomplishments pursuant to Section 316 of RA No. 7160 particularly on the unutilized ₱5.05 million project fund recorded under the General and the Trust Fund books, and submit the report thereon to the LCE as basis in decision making.

We recommended that the LCE fast track the implementation of projects that should have been completed in the first semester.  

Also, require the MPDO to submit monitoring report on its status of implementation, and use the same as basis in making decision in project implementation in accordance with the approved timetable/schedule embodied in the Annual Budget.

Likewise, the implementation of CY 2019 20% DF projects must be fast tracked so that benefits therefrom could be realized the earliest possible time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Development Council ensure that the projects funded from the 20% DF are development projects classified under Sections 2.3 and 3.0 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1, and will contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcome for the locality.

We also recommended deferment of future disbursement charged to 20% DF for projects incorporated in the current and continuing appropriations which are not classified as developmental projects under the subject JMC pending justifications thereof to ensure that funds are disbursed only for its intended purpose.  

Henceforth, financial transactions should be scrutinized carefully to ensure that only items that are directly associated in the implementation of developmental projects are properly charged to the 20% DF to maximize its utilization for more beneficial purpose.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to:

1.	Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor on the updated data and information of real Property Taxes for proper implementation of prescribed remedies and actions to generate more collections; and

require the Municipal Treasurer to:

for proper implementation of prescribed remedies

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:


      a) create an Inventory Committee which will conduct the actual  count
         of all supplies and materials on stock;


      b) submit to the Commission on Audit the properly  accomplished  RPCI
         not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for  the  first
         and second semesters, respectively;


      c) require  the  Municipal  Accountant  and  Municipal  Treasurer  to
         maintain SLCs and stock cards, respectively, for all receipts  and
         issuances of supplies/goods; and


      d) reconcile  the  Inventory  Accounts  per  books  with  the  actual
         supplies at hand.

We recommended that Management:


     a. instruct the Inventory  Committee  to  include  all  properties  in
        physical count and submit  an  updated  and  properly  accomplished
        RPCPPE with all the needed information;


     b. required the Property Officer  to  keep  property  cards  for  each
        category of PPE for control purposes;  and  see  to  it  that  this
        record contains adequate details/description of the properties such
        as model, serial  number,  location,  cost  and  other  information
        necessary to facilitate identification and reconciliation;


     c. direct the Municipal Accountant to completely account for each item
        composing the PPE, investigate on the discrepancy noted and  effect
        the necessary adjustments; and


     d. order both the Accounting Division and Property  Section  to  exert
        all efforts to reconcile their records on a regular basis  so  that
        discrepancies thereon are immediately corrected.

We recommended that Management:


     a. ensure that clothing allowance be appropriated  as  an  item  under
        Personal Services (PS) subject to the PS limitations in LGU budgets
        pursuant to Sections 325(a) and 331(b) of  R.A.  No.  7160  of  the
        “Local Government Code of 1991”;

     b. strictly comply with the rules and  regulations  on  the  grant  of
        Clothing Allowance; and


     c. stop the practice of utilizing the budget for office  supplies  for
        employees’ clothing allowance.

We recommended that Management ensure that attendance to  seminars  or
trainings be attended by only one or two officials/employees to  effectively
manage the meager resources of the Municipality  particularly  the  budgeted
amount for travel or training.

We recommended that Management:


     a. direct the official drivers be re-oriented of their duties  and  be
        required to prepare and submit fully accomplished serially numbered
        Driver’s Trip Tickets (DTT), for each trip and ensure that all  the
        information required by the prescribed format of the DTT should  be
        indicated, such as the dates of the  travels,  specific  places  of
        destinations, specific purpose of the trip,  names  of  passengers,
        speedometer readings, signatures of passengers, etc,;


     b. require the Municipal Accountant to defer  processing  of  payments
        for fuel consumption if these are not complied with;


     c. direct the officer in charge of gasoline to maintain  a  monitoring
        record of all requisitions and issuances  of  gasoline  and  submit
        Monthly Report of Official  Travels  and  Monthly  Report  of  Fuel
        Consumption of government motor transportation to the Auditor; and


     d. direct all concerned officials to comply with the  requirements  of
        COA  Circular  No.  77-61  dated  September  26,   1977   on   fuel
        consumptions and official travels.

We recommended that Management require  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
examine the  completeness  of  the  supporting  documents  to  ensure  their
validity, propriety and legality and avoid suspension  of  disbursements  in
audit.

We recommended that Management effect the increase of the fidelity
bond of the Disbursing Officer commensurate to her accountabilities, in
accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009, in order to
provide adequate protection on the Municipality’s resources.

We recommended that Management require the Office  of  the  Municipal
Accountant to:

a) ensure that transactions are properly recorded in  order  to  arrive  at
   accurate and reliable accounting data; and
b) make the following accounting entries for the transfer of funds:


     a. Other Funds


   Dr. Subsidy  to  General  Fund  Proper  /Special  Account  (5-02-14-070)
   ₱xxx
             Cr.   Cash   in   Bank   –   Local   Currency    (1-01-02-010)
   ₱xxx
    To record fund transfer from Other Funds to the General Fund


     b. General Fund



   Dr.    Cash    in    Bank     –     Local     Currency     (1-01-02-010)
₱xxx
              Cr.     Subsidy     from     Other     Fund     (4-03-02-040)
₱xxx
    To record fund transfer from Other Funds to the General Fund

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

   a. transfer the unutilized fund from previous years to  Trust  Liability-
      DRRM account in the Trust Fund books;


   b. include all the  information  needed  in  the  preparation  of  MDRRMF
      Utilization Report;


   c. prepare Subsidiary Ledgers as required under Section 5.1.12; and


   d. disclose in the Notes to  the  Financial  Statements  the  amount  and
      details of the unexpended balance of MDRRMF as of year-end.

We recommended that the LCE direct all employees with unliquidated cash advances to settle immediately cash advances for official travels in accordance with Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 97-002.  See to it that all cash advances are liquidated at year-end.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all concerned personnel to comply with the preparation of records/reports prescribed under COA Circular 2015-008.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to review immediately the balances of the inter agency payable accounts to determine the LGU’s actual liability to the concerned government agencies, and prepare the appropriate adjustments.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and the Implementing Departments to confirm whether the projects charged to particular trust funds were actually completed and determine whether there are any other outstanding obligations chargeable to the funds. If projects were already completed and no other outstanding obligations accrue to the funds, return immediately the unexpended balance to source agency. If the SA cannot be identified, remit immediately the remaining idle and unutilized funds to the Bureau of Treasury.
For prior year unreconciled balances, the Municipal Accountant shall continue to conduct reconciliation and effect the necessary adjustments in the financial statements

We recommended  that the LCE, being the Head of the Local School Board direct the Municipal General Services Officer and the BAC to observe that procurement of goods should be done through public bidding; other modes of procurement as provided under R. A. 9184 could be resorted to if the public bidding is not applicable. All transactions pertaining to procurement should be evaluated by the Bids and Awards Committee, and a corresponding resolution thereto should be prepared and attached to the corresponding disbursement voucher in consonance with the provision of Section 12 of RA No. 9184.
Also, we recommended that the LCE require the Accountant not to process disbursement vouchers relative to procurement transactions without the necessary documents and approved BAC resolutions to ensure that all transactions have undergone BAC evaluation.

We recommended that the LCE strictly direct the BAC Secretariat to prepare a consolidated and approved APP in compliance with Sections 7.3.4, 7.3.5 and 14.1(i) of RA No. 9184 to facilitate the procurement process and delivery of goods, services to end-users and immediate implementation of infrastructure projects for the benefit of intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Local Finance Committee to conduct a semi-annual review and general examination of expenses and accomplishments pursuant to Section 316 of RA No. 7160 particularly on the unutilized ₱5.05 million project fund recorded under the General and the Trust Fund books, and submit the report thereon to the LCE as basis in decision making.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Chief of the BCISWMP to prepare a comprehensive work plan for SWM operation indicating all activities needed to efficiently and effectively perform SWM functions with corresponding budget per activity.  This would more or less guide the LGU in the implementation and in assessing the SWM operations.
We also recommended to institutionalize the Solid Waste Management program by creating a separate office with separate budget and with permanent appointed personnel as Head of Office to run its affairs.

We recommended that the LCE fast track the implementation of projects that should have been completed in the first semester.  Also, require the MPDO to submit monitoring report on its status of implementation, and use the same as basis in making decision in project implementation in accordance with the approved timetable/schedule embodied in the Annual Budget.
Likewise, the implementation of CY 2019 20% DF projects must be fast tracked so that benefits therefrom could be realized the earliest possible time.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Development Council ensure that the projects funded from the 20% DF are development projects classified under Sections 2.3 and 3.0 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1, and will contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcome for the locality.
We also recommended deferment of future disbursement charged to 20% DF for projects incorporated in the current and continuing appropriations which are not classified as developmental projects under the subject JMC pending justifications thereof to ensure that funds are disbursed only for its intended purpose.  Henceforth, financial transactions should be scrutinized carefully to ensure that only items that are directly associated in the implementation of developmental projects are properly charged to the 20% DF to maximize its utilization for more beneficial purpose.

We commended Management for the substantial implementation of projects in compliance with complying Rule 1, Sections 3 (e) and Rule 5, Section 4(1) of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 10121.  However, we recommend that Management should strive for total implementation of all projects embodied in the 5% LDRRM Fund.

We commended Management and the Local School Board for the substantial implementation of PPAs funded by the Special Education Fund, thus, ensuring that the intended benefits were derived therefrom and enjoyed by the school populace.  However, we also recommended that Management strive for total implementation of the PPAs as embodied in the Annual School Board Budget.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to:
Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor on the updated data and information of real Property Taxes for proper implementation of prescribed remedies and actions to generate more collections; and
Prepare and submit regularly a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies to the Sanggunian for the body to assist in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.
We recommended that the Municipal Administrator forward the pertinent documents to the Municipal Accountant for her to cause the recording of the building in the books of the Municipality of Bato.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the payables as to the source agencies. Henceforth, record receipts and disbursements of fund transfers in the appropriate account as prescribed under the Revised Chart of Accounts for proper monitoring of transactions relating thereto.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Disbursement Vouchers for the return of the excess fund from completed projects, including funds from projects which the LGU finds difficult to implement, to the National Government, through the Bureau of Treasury and to the General Fund account of the LGU, in the case of the unutilized LGU counterpart.
We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer complete the projects funded by the National Government within the timeframe provided for in the MOA.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit to the Municipal Accountant an updated certified list of the uncollected business permit fees and space rentals so that the Municipal Accountant can record the transactions in the books of the LGU.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, together with the Municipal Development Council, through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator:
Identify and prioritize projects which are considered vital and urgent to the Municipality and include in the AIP only those that can be implemented during the year through proper scheduling; and,
Review the unimplemented projects in prior years funded from the Continuing Appropriations of 20% DF during budget preparation to determine its viability and necessity for inclusion in the AIP, otherwise, recommend reversion of the funds if no longer needed.
We also recommend that the Municipal Engineer prepare the Program of Works of Projects of projects identified as priority by the MDC so that sufficient funds will be appropriated for each project and ensure complete implementation.
We further recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer, stop the practice of reverting appropriations unless the amounts are balances of continuing appropriations which are no longer needed, and augment funds only if the same is supported with an SB Ordinance.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer prepare the Statement of Work Accomplished every month for every infrastructure projects implemented by administration to be validated and reported by the Municipality’s  Inspectorate Committee. Also, issue the Certificate of Completion when projects are completed, noted by the Municipal Mayor, supported with the Inspection Report.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the Statement of Work Accomplished, Inspection Report and the Certificate of Completion for all infrastructure projects implemented by administration before payments for labor are effected.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
implement austerity measures in the use of LDRRMF of LGU-Bato and refrain the conduct of seminars and trainings outside Region VIII to prevent incurrence of unnecessary expenditures; and
include in the LDRRMF Investment Plan the procurement of standby food packs and hygiene kits so that these are readily prepared and stored under defined conditions with good storage practices.
We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant withhold the applicable tax rate from the honoraria and allowances given to Resource Speakers and employees of other government agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, incorporate in the SWM Plan the conduct of necessary training for SWM Personnel to ensure that solid wastes are properly handled and that the risk of hazards to them and to the public are avoided or minimized.

We recommended that the Local GFPS formulate a GAD Code for the Municipality of Bato and submit the same to the Sangguniang Bayan for the issuance of the appropriate ordinance.
We also recommended that the Municipal Planning & Development Officer spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD Database of LGU-Bato.
We further recommended that the GAD Focal Person incorporate in their GAD Plan and Budget the establishment of a fully equipped lactation station and ensure the accomplishment of the same.
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor cause the creation of an Internal Audit Services in the organizational structure of the Municipality of Bato.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:


   a. require the  Municipal  Accountant  to  send  demand  letters  to  the
      concerned Accountable Officers (AOs) for the immediate liquidation  of
      the cash advance or settlement of the amounts due from them;


   b. suspend the salaries of erring accountable officers  until  such  time
      that full settlement of their cash advances are effected;


   c. stop the practice of granting cash advances to officials and employees
      unless the previous ones are fully liquidated; and


   d. ensure that rules and regulations on the grant of  cash  advances  are
      strictly observed.

We recommended that Management require the  Municipal  Accountant  to:
(a) adopt the Perpetual Inventory System in recording purchases of  supplies
for  stock;  (b)  record  receipt  of  supplies/materials  by  debiting  the
appropriate inventory account and not directly as  expense;  (c)   base  the
recording of the corresponding supplies expense accounts on the  Summary  of
Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) together with the original copy of  the
Requisition and Issuance Slip (RIS); and (d)   compute the cost of  supplies
issued and ending inventory using the moving average method.

We recommended that Management:


       a. instruct the Inventory Committee  to  include  all  properties  in
          physical count and submit an  updated  and  properly  accomplished
          RPCPPE with all the needed information;


       b. direct the Property  Officer  to  keep  property  cards  for  each
          category of PPE for control purposes; and  see  to  it  that  this
          record contains adequate  details/description  of  the  properties
          such as model, serial number, location, cost and other information
          necessary to facilitate identification and reconciliation; and


       c. require both the Accounting Division and Property Section to exert
          all efforts to reconcile their records on a regular basis so  that
          discrepancies thereon are immediately corrected.

We recommended that Management:

a. require strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No.  2009-001
   regarding the submission of Purchase Orders and  contracts  within  five
   (5) days from execution thereof;

b. put a stop to the practice  of  using  unauthorized  “receiving  stamps”
   bearing the name “Commission on Audit”;


c. notify the Auditor of the deliveries of procured  items  within  twenty-
   four (24) hours from acceptance in adherence to the provision of COA Cir
   No. 95-006.

We recommended that Management  require  the  BAC  and  the  concerned
officers and employees to:


   a. strictly comply with the provisions enumerated in the Revised  IRR  of
      RA No. 9184; and


   b. exercise due diligence in the performance of their  duties  to  ensure
      that all requirements of the procurement law  are  faithfully  adhered
      to.

We recommended the following:
a. 	The Local Chief Executive (LCE) reorganize the Inventory Committee and require the same to conduct physical inventory and render report thereon as required under Section 124, Chapter 7, Volume I of the NGAS Manual. The progress of the inventory taking should be monitored by the LCE;
b. 	Thereafter, the Committee, the General Services Office (GSO) and the Municipal Accounting Office reconcile all the inventory reports with the books of accounts; and
c. Require the designated Property Officer to maintain property cards and update them periodically to aid in the conduct of physical count and to solve or minimize the perennial problem of unreconciled Accounting and Property records.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
Designated General Services Officer to consolidate the weekly RIS  in the SSMI for CY 2015 and previous years transactions, and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant together with the original copies of RIS; and
Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary JEVs based on the submitted SSMIs and record the expenditures under the Prior Period Adjustment account pursuant to Section 121 of NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to expedite the immediate return to the General Fund the excess amount of ₱2.684 million erroneously transferred to the TF for the immediate appropriation thereof to programs, projects and activities intended for the delivery of basic services to the people.
Henceforth, we recommended that in determining the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF to be transferred to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability-DRRM’ (Code 438) in the Trust Fund books, the Municipal Budget Officer shall consider only the unexpended/unappropriated balances of the programs in the General Fund.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to confer with the Philippine National Bank and request for confirmation if the aforecited Local Currency Savings Accounts are actually existing per bank records.  If confirmed positively, request for bank statements and prepare the BRS. Otherwise, require the Municipal Accountant to effect adjustments in the books in accordance with pertinent provisions of COA Circular 97-001 dated February 5, 2015.

We recommended that the Municipal Vice-Mayor direct all members of the sangguniang bayan including himself to liquidate immediately cash advances for official travels in accordance with Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 97-002.  We also recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to disapprove grant of additional cash advances to LGU officers and employees including other officials of National Government Agencies who are allowed to charge cash advances to the LGU fund without settling first previous outstanding cash advances.  See to it that all cash advances are liquidated at year-end.

Meanwhile, we also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer identify the cause/s of the over-deposit of collections amounting to ₱255.57.  If proven to be as such, require the Municipal Accountant to recognize liability, otherwise the over-deposit of collections shall be receipted by the Municipal Treasurer in favor of the LGU-Bato.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning Officer as the LGU focal Official in charge with the preparation of the 20% Economic Development Plan formulate a comprehensive plan in which the accomplishments thereof are achievable during the budget year.
We also recommended that the LCE require the MPDO to submit monitoring report on the status of 20% EDF implementation, and use the same as basis in making decision in project implementations in accordance with the approved timetable/schedule embodied in the Annual Budget.
In addition, fast track the implementation of the CY 2018 20% Economic Development Projects so that benefits therefrom could be realized in the earliest possible time.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to:
Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor on the updated data and information of real Property Taxes for proper implementation of prescribed remedies and actions to generate more collections; and
Prepare and submit regularly a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies to the Sanggunian for the body to assist in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.

We recommended to the Management to fast track the implementation of the CY 2018 and prior year’s project so that benefits therefrom could be realized the earliest possible time.
Likewise, compliance with Rule 1, Section 3 (e) and Rule 5, Section 4(1) of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 10121 is enjoined.

Municipal Budget Officer, as the LGU focal Official in charge with the preparation of the Annual Budget, observe the time frame in the budget preparation, scrutinize and review the details and attached supporting documents in the Annual Budget so that errors in the position descriptions and supporting documents to the programs are avoided.

Ascertain that the implementation of the CY 2017 20% Economic Development Projects are fast tracked so that benefits therefrom could be realized in the earliest possible time.

collections  at least once a week, or as  soon  as  the  collections  reach

purposes and avoid the loss of funds  thru  misuse.  Stop  immediately  the
practice  of  retaining  large  amount  of  collections  in  his   custody,
otherwise, appropriate sanctions will  be  imposed  on  him  for  continued
disregard of existing regulations

collections are withheld in the custody of said official.

books of the agency.  Review previous records to  establish  the  source  of

completed programs/projects and refund the unutilized  amounts  as  required

and project coordinators to  implement  immediately  the  two  programs  and

Management Board to prepare a SWM Plan with emphasis on  the  implementation

¢.  stop the practice of granting cash advances to officials and employees

balance; and (b) provide full details and supporting documents for all the
adjustments made to the government equity. )

with all the collectors and liguidating officer to re-orient them of their

Municipal Accountant relative to the remittance, reporting and
accounting of collections; and

¢.  see to it that the above practice will not be committed again and ensure
that collectors found to commit the same shall mot be allowed to

expenses for P8 for the subsequent fiscal year will not violate the provisions for the PS

d. Require the designated Property Officer to maintain property cards and update
